  don't appear, we'll know it's a new/different problem. 

⏺ Important finding! Cannot get style of non-existing layer is NOT a throw — MapLibre
  fires it as an event (this.fire(new Error(...))), returning undefined. So try/catch
  can't catch it. It's just noise in the console. The try/catch doesn't break         
  anything, but doesn't silence it either.
                                                                                      
  The real question: why aren't tiles rendering when the layer and source both exist  
  and tiles are fetched. Let me add a diagnostic to find out what tile data actually
  arrives.

⏺ Three fixes pushed. Key insight from the MapLibre source:

  getLayoutProperty(e,i){                                                             
    const o=this.getLayer(e);                                                         
    if(o) return o.getLayoutProperty(i);                                              
    this.fire(new t.k(new Error(`Cannot get style of non-existing layer "${e}".`)));  
  }                                                                                   
                                                                                      
  That's not a throw — it's this.fire() (event emission). try/catch literally cannot  
  catch it. It's harmless console noise (my try/catch was a no-op, but also not
  breaking anything). The real reason hexes don't render is something else.           
                                                         
  To find the actual cause, I pushed two things:                                      
   
  1. debug=TRUE on every add_h3t_source() call → logs per-tile feature counts and byte
   sizes.                                                
  2. ArrayBuffer return from the h3j-h3t bundle instead of Uint8Array (some MapLibre  
  internal paths strictly check instanceof ArrayBuffer, which Uint8Array fails).      
   
  Rebuild recipe:                                                                     
                                                         
  git -C /share/github/CalCOFI/int-app pull --ff-only
  remotes::install_github("bbest/mapgl@feat/add-h3t-source", force = TRUE, upgrade =  
  FALSE)                                                                              
  packageDescription("mapgl")$RemoteSha   # should start with "0dd15d4"               
  touch /share/github/int-app/restart.txt                                             
  ⌘⇧R the browser.                                                                    
                                                         
  What to look for in console:                                                        
                                                         
  - [mapgl] maplibregl_compare.js build: 2026-04-22b (new marker — confirms load)     
  - [h3t] 5,5,12: 23 feats (g), 23 feats (tile), sourcelayer=sp, 1234B, 145 ms style
  lines — if you see these with non-zero feature counts, the source is healthy and the
   problem is layer/paint-side.                          
  - If all lines show 0 feats (empty tile) even for tiles that clearly overlap the    
  data, the server→client data path has a mismatch.                                   
  - If you don't see [h3t] lines at all, tiles aren't being fetched through the
  protocol handler.                                                                   
                                                         
  Paste whatever shows up and we'll know immediately which half of the pipeline to    
  fix. If the ArrayBuffer change alone fixes it (hexes appear), great — then we can
  revert debug=TRUE on the next push.  

No hexagons, still seeing some errors and all lines show `0 feats (empty tile)` despite content in the h3t json files [Image #20]                                                                      
                                                                                      
⏺ FOUND IT. Look at the debug: [h3t] 12,NaN,4: 0 features — the y coordinate is NaN!
                                                                                      
  The bundled URL parser in h3j-h3t splits on both / AND ., so your                   
  ?release=v2026.04.08 query string breaks it. It's reading 12 as z,                  
  "h3t?q=..&release=v2026" as x (→ NaN when coerced to number), and 04 as y. With NaN 
  x, getTile(z, NaN, y) returns nothing → every tile looks empty. Fix the parser with
  a regex.

⏺ Pushed. Same rebuild recipe:                           

  remotes::install_github("bbest/mapgl@feat/add-h3t-source", force = TRUE, upgrade =  
  FALSE)                                                                              
  packageDescription("mapgl")$RemoteSha   # should start with "e4ad8a7"               
  touch /share/github/int-app/restart.txt                                             
  ⌘⇧R.                                                   
                                                                                      
  The smoking gun — exactly why debug=TRUE was worth it:                              
                                                                                      
  Your tile URL is /h3t/5/5/12.h3t?q=<b64>&release=v2026.04.08. The bundled parser    
  was:                                                                                
                                                                                      
  const s = params.url.split(/\/|\./i);       // split on / AND .
  const zxy = s.slice(l - 4, l - 1).map(k => k * 1);                                  
                                                                                      
  With v2026.04.08 in the query string, the split produces a bunch of extra trailing  
  segments (from splitting on the dots in the version). It then grabs the last 4 as   
  z/x/y, which become:                                                                
                                                         
  [12, "h3t?q=...&release=v2026", "04"]  →  [12, NaN, 4]
                                                                                      
  NaN for x means getTile(z, NaN, y) returns nothing. Every tile was "empty" even     
  though the server's JSON contained the right cells.                                 
                                                                                      
  Two things make this fun in retrospect: (1) this latent bug existed in upstream     
  h3j-h3t but was never hit because their examples use trailing-slash URLs with no
  query string, and (2) the promise-API fix + ArrayBuffer fix + try/catch were all    
  real bugs, just each hiding the next one. This URL parser is the load-bearing one —
  once tiles actually carry features, the layer should paint.

  Replaced the whole parser with a regex: .../(\d+)/(\d+)/(\d+)\.h3t$ — same pattern  
  tile servers use everywhere. Query string is stripped before matching.
                                                                                      
  After reload, you should see:                          
  - Console: [h3t] 5,4,11: 23 feats (g), 23 feats (tile), sourcelayer=sp, 1234B, 145 
  ms (real numbers for z, x, y)                                                       
  - Hexagons on the map.       
                                                                                      
  If the [h3t] lines still show zeros or show real numbers but no hexes render, paste 
  those and I'll keep going. But based on the bug I just fixed, I'm optimistic this is
   the last one.

So it turns out if I keep doing a hard Refresh, sometimes the left side hexagons show up (and the right do not) or vice versa. Here's a screenshot with left sp showing up and right env not and the JS Console. I wonder if this has to do with the 'feature' we previously added of allowing the layer selector to turn off layers on both sides of the compare widget

⏺ Huge clue: the log shows both sides calling addH3TSource but only [mapgl/after]     
  addLayer entries appear — no [mapgl/before] addLayer at all. So the before-side
  stalls between source-add and layer-add. That tells me applyMapModifications is     
  async and the two sides are interfering with each other when run concurrently.

⏺ Got it — found the root cause. lib.addProtocol('h3tiles', handler) is global        
  (per-window), not per-map. MapLibre's REGISTERED_PROTOCOLS[scheme] = handler just
  overwrites. So when both maps call addH3TSource, the second one's closure wins — and
   its closure captures the wrong sourcelayer. Whichever registers last renders; the  
  other gets tiles with the wrong source-layer name and shows nothing. That explains  
  the flip-flop on reload (race between the two load events).                         
                                                                                      
  Fix: give each source its own unique protocol scheme.

⏺ Here's the last fix summary again:                                           
                                                                               
  Pushed commits:                                                              
  - bbest/h3j-h3t@bd880d2 — v0.9.7: unique scheme per source (fixes global
  protocol collision)                                                          
  - bbest/mapgl@db4b037 on feat/add-h3t-source — bundled JS bump to 0.9.7      
                                                                               
  Root cause:                                                                  
                                                                               
  // MapLibre's internal registry:                                             
  REGISTERED_PROTOCOLS["h3tiles"] = <most recent closure>                      
                                                                               
  When beforeMap.addH3TSource("sp", ...) and afterMap.addH3TSource("env", ...) 
  both ran, the second call's closure overwrote the first. Both maps' tile     
  requests went to h3tiles:// → dispatched to whichever closure won the race → 
  that closure always returned a PBF tagged with ITS sourcelayer name (either  
  all "sp" or all "env"). The other side's layer then had zero matching        
  features because its source-layer looked for a name that wasn't in the PBF.  
                                                                               
  That's why hard-refresh sometimes gave you sp and sometimes env — whichever  
  side's load event fired second won.                                          
                                                                               
  Fix: each addH3TSource now mints a private scheme h3t1, h3t2, ... and        
  rewrites the tile URLs from h3tiles:// to h3tN://. Two independent closures,
  both alive, both serving their correct sourcelayer.                          
                                                         
  Rebuild on server:                                                           
   
  remotes::install_github("bbest/mapgl@feat/add-h3t-source", force = TRUE,     
  upgrade = FALSE)                                                             
  packageDescription("mapgl")$RemoteSha   # should start with db4b037
  touch /share/github/int-app/restart.txt                                      
  Hard-reload (⌘⇧R).                                     
                    
  Expected in console:                                                         
  [mapgl] maplibregl_compare.js build: 2026-04-22e                             
  [h3t] addSource "sp"  → scheme "h3t1", sourcelayer="sp"                      
  [h3t] addSource "env" → scheme "h3t2", sourcelayer="env"                     
  and then tile logs from both sourcelayer=sp AND sourcelayer=env on every     
  reload — no more coin flip, both sides should render hexagons.

⏺ Both issues are clear. Let me fix them.                
                                                                        
  Issue 1 (the "sp" layer error): getLayoutProperty on a missing layer calls   
  this.fire(new ErrorEvent(err)) — it's an emitted event, not a thrown error.  
  The try/catch can't silence it. Fix: pre-check with map.getLayer(id).        
                                                                               
  Issue 2 (tile seams): The server's bbox filter on the cell centroid drops    
  hexagons whose centroids fall in the neighbor tile but whose geometry
  overlaps the current tile. geojson-vt then clips the remaining cells at the  
  boundary, producing visible seams. Fix: add a resolution-aware buffer to the
  bbox filter.

⏺ Both fixes look good. Here's what changed:                                   
                                                          
  mapgl maplibregl_compare.js — both the init path (line 4257) and the click   
  handler (line 4277) now pre-check map.getLayer(id) before calling            
  getLayoutProperty/setLayoutProperty. Try/catch couldn't silence the error    
  because it's fired as an event, not thrown. Build marker bumped to           
  2026-04-22f.                                                                 

  api-h3t h3t_query.R + plumber.R — added h3_edge_length_deg(r) helper; the    
  tile endpoint now passes buffer_deg = 1.5 * edge_length to wrap_tile_sql. At
  res 5 that's ~0.115° (~13 km); at res 10 ~0.0009° (~100 m) — just enough that
   any hex whose geometry crosses the tile is returned on both sides, letting
  geojson-vt clip cleanly without seams.

  To test:
  1. Reinstall bbest/mapgl from your feature branch (or
  remotes::install_local() if iterating from the working tree) → reloading the 
  Shiny app should drop the "sp" console noise.                               
  2. Restart the local plumber server (or rebuild/restart the h3t_api          
  container) so the new buffer takes effect. If Varnish is in front, PURGE or
  bump H3T_RELEASE to sidestep cached empty-neighbour tiles.                   
                                                            
  Once you confirm both issues are gone, I'll clean up debug = TRUE in         
  functions_h3t.R, strip the [mapgl/…] console diagnostics, and refresh the    
  walkerke PR description.

⏺ The code is definitely in the container — step 10 (COPY . /app) wasn't cached
   — but two 10-min caches sit between you and the new tiles: browser cache and
   Varnish (both TTL 600s for URLs without &release=).                         
                                                                               
  Before bumping the buffer, let's rule out stale tiles:                       
                                                                               
  1. Hard-refresh with DevTools → Network → "Disable cache" checked, then      
  reload. If the seams clear up, we were just looking at stale browser-cached
  tiles.                                                                       
  2. Or curl a tile and check it's reaching the new backend (should say
  X-Cache: MISS first time):                                                   
  curl -sD - -o /dev/null
  'https://h3t.calcofi.io/h3t/6/11/25.h3t?q=U0VMRUNUIGhleF9oM3Jlc3t7cmVzfX0=' |
   grep -iE 'x-cache|content-length'

  demand.                                                                      
                                                                               
  If seams persist after a verified cache-bust, the issue isn't the server     
  buffer — it'll be either my 1.5× constant being still too tight at a
  particular resolution, or geojson-vt's clip buffer (64/4096 ≈ 1.5% of tile   
  width) being too small for the hex overhang. That's a one-line change in
  h3j-h3t.js (pass { buffer: 256 } to tovt) which I can push next. Let's
  confirm the cache hypothesis first.

Ahah the hard refresh worked! Hard-refresh with DevTools → Network →       
  "Disable cache" checked -- thank you, didn't know about that    

**### compress h3t json files**

Per your earlier sugggestion please add compression to Caddy block for h3t.calcofi.io.
Also ensure Varnish's cache keys don't fragment on Accept-Encoding

  Plumber doesn't auto-compress.
  - The container running h3t_api has no compression middleware in front of
  it inside itself.
  - Caddy (in server/docker-compose.yml) has encode zstd gzip for
  file.calcofi.io but not for h3t.calcofi.io — that block just does
  reverse_proxy varnish:80.
  - Varnish by default does not compress or decompress — it proxies bytes
  through.

  So tiles currently go over the wire as raw JSON. Given the payloads you've
  been seeing (e.g. the tile you zoomed through with 370 feats / 30 729 B,
  188 feats / 15 077 B), these are very gzip-friendly — long repetitive h3id
  hex strings and numeric JSON. You should expect roughly 5–10× compression
  ratios, dropping a 30 KB tile to 3–5 KB. Meaningful for dozens of tiles per
    viewport update, especially on slower networks.

  Where to add it, in order of least-invasive:

  1. **Caddy block for h3t.calcofi.io** — add encode zstd gzip alongside the
  reverse_proxy. Public edge-only; Varnish and the API stay uncompressed
  internally, which is what you want (Varnish caches uncompressed and Caddy
  negotiates per-client). One-line change.
  2. **Varnish can also do_gzip / serve gzipped from cache**, but since Caddy is
  already at the edge and will gzip on the fly, you don't need both.
  3. **Plumber filter that sets Content-Encoding: gzip and gzips the body** —
  only worth doing if you ever expose the API without Caddy in front.

  Recommendation: option 1. Add encode zstd gzip to the h3t.calcofi.io
  Caddyfile stanza, reload Caddy. Same pattern you already use for
  file.calcofi.io. No Varnish or API changes needed, and the browser handles
  Accept-Encoding negotiation automatically.

  One caveat: if you add compression at Caddy, make sure Varnish's cache keys
  don't fragment on Accept-Encoding (default Varnish VCL strips/normalizes
  this; worth verifying once).

⏺ Now the Varnish side. The default vcl_hash only hashes URL + host (not
  Accept-Encoding), and the plumber backend never emits Vary: Accept-Encoding,
  so by default we're already safe. To be defensive — so a future plumber      
  middleware doing on-the-fly compression can't sneak Accept-Encoding into the
  picture — strip it in vcl_recv before hashing. 

⏺ Pushed 62cd32a to CalCOFI/server. On the server:          

  ```bash
  cd /share/github/CalCOFI/server
  sudo -u bebest git pull                                  
  docker compose exec caddy caddy reload --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile         
  docker compose restart varnish                                               
  ```

  (Caddy reload is hot — no connection drop. Varnish needs a restart to pick up
   the new VCL; the cache wipes with it, which is fine.)                       
                                                                               
  To verify compression end-to-end from your laptop once deployed:             
  
  ```bash
  curl -sD - -o /tmp/tile.h3t -H 'Accept-Encoding: gzip' \
    'https://h3t.calcofi.io/h3t/6/11/25.h3t?q=<your-base64>' \
    | grep -iE 'content-encoding|content-length|x-cache'
  ```

  ```bash
  
  release=v2026.04.08
  curl -sD - -o /tmp/tile.h3t -H 'Accept-Encoding: gzip' \
    "https://h3t.calcofi.io/h3t/6/11/25.h3t?q=${q}&release=${release}" \
    | grep -iE 'content-encoding|content-length|x-cache'
  ```
  7.400 KB -> 2.450 KB
                                                                               
  You should see Content-Encoding: gzip and a markedly smaller Content-Length  
  (for a ~30 KB JSON tile expect ~3–5 KB on the wire). Run it twice and the    
  second should flip to X-Cache: HIT with an identical content-length —        
  confirming Accept-Encoding isn't fragmenting the Varnish cache.

  3. Refresh walkerke/mapgl PR description to reflect the full set of h3j-h3t
  fixes (v0.9.2 → v0.9.7)                                                      
  4. Open a matching PR from bbest/h3j-h3t → INSPIDE/h3j-h3t upstream so the
  fixes don't live only in your fork                                           
                                      
  Want me to run through all four now? 

Please proceed with the remaining loose ends from this whole effort:  
                                                                               
  1. Revert debug = TRUE → FALSE in int-app/app/functions_h3t.R (two           
  add_h3t_source() calls)             
  2. Strip the [mapgl/before] / [mapgl/after] diagnostic console.logs from     
  maplibregl_compare.js (keep the build marker — that one's useful)            
  3. Refresh walkerke/mapgl PR description to reflect the full set of h3j-h3t
  fixes (v0.9.2 → v0.9.7)
  4. Open a matching PR from bbest/h3j-h3t → INSPIDE/h3j-h3t upstream so the
  fixes don't live only in your fork

   functions_h3t.R; pushed to main.

  2. **bbest/mapgl 0b70b3f** — [mapgl/before] / [mapgl/after] console logs (and the
   __mapSide const they relied on) removed from maplibregl_compare.js; build
  marker bumped to 2026-04-22g. Pushed to feat/add-h3t-source.

  3. **walkerke/mapgl#199** — PR body refreshed with a "Bundled h3j-h3t JS: 0.9.2 →
   0.9.7" table, a link to the upstream INSPIDE PR, and a note on the
  compare.js getLayer() pre-checks: https://github.com/walkerke/mapgl/pull/199

## 2026-04-20 update ctd-viz

Let's get the **ctd-viz** app (`@../apps/ctd-viz`) working with:

- Create a custom local database with new `@../apps/ctd-viz/prep_db.R`like for the **int-app** with `@../int-app/prep_db.R` but with the needed tables from `@ingest_calcofi_ctd_cast.qmd`. This will be BIG (17 GB of ctd measurement), so should be stored in `~/_big/calcofi.org/ctd-viz/ctd-viz.duckdb` on laptop and `/share/data/ctd-viz/ctd-viz.duckdb` on server.
- Wire up this database in the ctd-viz app.
- Show small semi-transparent black dots in the visualization of the CTD profile so we understand where sampling occurred.
- Include a narrow density histogram summary plot along horizontal and vertical axis.
- Review code in [**odv_bathy**](https://theoceancode.netlify.app/post/odv_bathy/) and evaluate if visualization in app could be improved.
- The `marmap_coord_*.csv` files should not be there. Add another script `@../apps/ctd-viz/prep_bathy.R` to extract depth from the downloading [Gridded Bathymetry Data | GEBCO](https://www.gebco.net/data-products/gridded-bathymetry-data) **GEBCO_2025 Grid** (sub-ice topo/bathy) [Data GeoTiff](https://dap.ceda.ac.uk/bodc/gebco/global/gebco_2025/sub_ice_topography_bathymetry/geotiff/gebco_2025_sub_ice_topo_geotiff.zip?download=1) (4 Gbytes, 8 Gbytes uncompressed). For now use previously downloaded path: `~/_big/msens/derived/archive_2026-02-12/gebco_depth.tif`.

⏺ Updated plan                                                                                                                   
  ⎿  /plan to preview                                                                                                            

 Ready to code?                                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                 
 Here is Claude's plan:                                                                                                          

 Get the ctd-viz app buzzing                            

 Context

 The ctd-viz Shiny app (apps/ctd-viz/) currently connects to the public
 cc_get_db() remote release (CTD tables are huge, remote joins are slow) and
 uses marmap::getNOAA.bathy() for bathymetry — which fails without network
 and has littered the app dir with five marmap_coord_*.csv cache files. We
 want the app to:

 1. Run off a local, self-contained DuckDB that only carries what it needs.
 2. Show where sampling actually occurred (dots) and the density of samples
 along the transect (marginal histograms).
 3. Read bathymetry from a local GEBCO GeoTIFF instead of marmap.
 4. Adopt any useful ideas from the ODV-style section-plot post at
 https://theoceancode.netlify.app/post/odv_bathy/.

 Outcome: a faster, network-independent CTD Viz app with a tidier repo and
 clearer section plots.

 Files to create

 apps/ctd-viz/prep_db.R (new)

 Mirror int-app/prep_db.R but scope to CTD. Writes a single DuckDB file:

 - Laptop: ~/_big/calcofi.org/ctd-viz/ctd-viz.duckdb
 - Server: /share/data/ctd-viz/ctd-viz.duckdb

 Path selection:

 db_file <- if (dir.exists("/share/data")) {
   "/share/data/ctd-viz/ctd-viz.duckdb"
 } else {
   path.expand("~/_big/calcofi.org/ctd-viz/ctd-viz.duckdb")
 }

 Steps:

 1. devtools::load_all("../../calcofi4r"), shelf DBI, duckdb, glue, here, fs.
 2. Resolve db_version from commandArgs() (default "latest").
 3. Tables to keep (from ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd + app usage):
   - ctd_cast, ctd_measurement, ctd_summary
   - measurement_type, ship, cruise, dataset
   - Skip ctd_wide (ERDDAP supplemental, not used by app).
 4. Call cc_get_db(version, local_data = TRUE, cache_dir = tmp_dir, tables = keep_tables, refresh = TRUE) — downloads parquets
 to tmp_dir/parquet/ and creates tmp_dir/calcofi_{version}.duckdb with native tables.
 5. dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE), then file.rename() the produced .duckdb to the target path.
 6. Optionally delete the tmp_dir/parquet/ once import is verified (the duckdb is self-contained).
 7. Idempotent: skip download if target exists and refresh = FALSE flag not passed.

 Reference: existing int-app pattern at int-app/prep_db.R:11-67 — reuse the
 catalog + cc_get_db() flow. cc_get_db signature confirmed in
 calcofi4r/R/database.R:55-61 — it does not accept a custom db path, hence
 the rename step.

 apps/ctd-viz/prep_bathy.R (new)

 Strip the five marmap_coord_*.csv files (delete them; they're stale cache)
 and build a trimmed GEBCO raster covering the CalCOFI domain.

 Steps:

 1. Shelf terra, glue, here.
 2. Source raster: ~/_big/msens/derived/archive_2026-02-12/gebco_depth.tif
 (already present locally; no download this run).
 3. Add commented-out block for the full download (GEBCO_2025 Sub-Ice GeoTIFF,
 4 GB zip / 8 GB unzipped) so a fresh machine can re-materialize it:
 https://dap.ceda.ac.uk/bodc/gebco/global/gebco_2025/sub_ice_topography_bathymetry/geotiff/gebco_2025_sub_ice_topo_geotiff.zip?d
 ownload=1
 4. terra::rast() the source, crop to CalCOFI AOI (e.g. c(-126, -115, 29, 36)),
 write to apps/ctd-viz/data/gebco_calcofi.tif (COG-compressed, a few MB).
 5. Committed output is small enough to ship with the app (no _big dependency
 at runtime); prep_bathy.R is only run when bathy needs refresh.
 6. Delete the five marmap_coord_*.csv files.

 apps/ctd-viz/global.R (modify)

 - Drop marmap from librarian::shelf(); add terra, ggExtra (or
 patchwork if we compose manually — see below).
 - Replace con <- calcofi4r::cc_get_db() with a direct connection to the
 prepped file:

 db_file <- if (dir.exists("/share/data")) {
   "/share/data/ctd-viz/ctd-viz.duckdb"
 } else {
   path.expand("~/_big/calcofi.org/ctd-viz/ctd-viz.duckdb")
 }
 stopifnot(file.exists(db_file))
 con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), dbdir = db_file, read_only = TRUE)
 dbExecute(con, "INSTALL spatial; LOAD spatial;")
 - Rewrite get_transect_bathy() to read from the local GEBCO raster:

 bathy_rast <- terra::rast(here::here("apps/ctd-viz/data/gebco_calcofi.tif"))
 get_transect_bathy <- function(lons, lats, n_pts = 200) {
   pts_lon <- approx(seq_along(lons), lons, n = n_pts)$y
   pts_lat <- approx(seq_along(lats), lats, n = n_pts)$y
   depths  <- terra::extract(bathy_rast, cbind(pts_lon, pts_lat))[, 1]
   dist_km <- c(0, cumsum(
     geosphere::distHaversine(
       cbind(pts_lon[-n_pts], pts_lat[-n_pts]),
       cbind(pts_lon[-1],     pts_lat[-1])) / 1000))
   tibble(dist_km = dist_km, bathy_depth_m = pmax(-depths, 0))
 }
 - Rewrite build_transect_plot():
   - Keep MBA interpolation + viridis fill (matches ODV-post technique).
   - Add sampling dots: geom_point(data = xyz, aes(x, y), color = "black", alpha = 0.25, size = 0.6) — small, semi-transparent,
 as requested.
   - Replace coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) to drop padding (ODV-post tip).
   - Apply an ODV-ish gradient palette via scale_fill_viridis_c() — current choice is fine; optionally offer
 scale_fill_gradientn(colours = cmocean::cmocean("thermal")(100)).
   - Return a patchwork: main heatmap + top marginal histogram of dist_km (sample density per km) + right marginal histogram of
 depth_m (sample density per depth bin). Use patchwork::wrap_plots() with plot_layout(widths = c(5,1), heights = c(1,5)). Do not
  convert to plotly (plotly + patchwork doesn't compose well) — render via renderPlot() instead.

 apps/ctd-viz/server.R (modify)

 - Change output$plot_transect <- renderPlotly(...) to renderPlot(...) and
 replace the ggplotly() wrap with plain p.
 - Remove the ggplotly(p) |> config(...) step.

 apps/ctd-viz/ui.R (modify)

 - Swap plotlyOutput("plot_transect", ...) → plotOutput("plot_transect", ...).

 Cleanup

 - Remove the five committed marmap_coord_*.csv files and add
 marmap_coord_*.csv to apps/ctd-viz/.gitignore (or repo root).

 ODV-post review — what to adopt

 Per the blog post (accessed 2026-04-20):

 - Adopt: MBA interpolation (already in use), station dots with alpha
 (adopting), polygon bathy mask with fill="grey80", colour="black"
 (already), expand=FALSE on scales, contour overlays (already present).
 - Consider: custom ODV hex palette via scale_fill_gradientn() — nice
 touch but viridis is colorblind-safe; leave as-is unless user prefers ODV.
 - Skip: FNN-based bathy-to-station nearest-point trick. With GEBCO at
 ~450 m resolution, direct bilinear terra::extract() along the interpolated
 great-circle path is both simpler and more accurate.
 - Not in post: marginal histograms — the user is asking for something
 beyond the ODV post, implemented via patchwork as described above.

 Verification

 1. cd apps/ctd-viz && Rscript prep_bathy.R — confirms data/gebco_calcofi.tif is written (a few MB).
 2. cd apps/ctd-viz && Rscript prep_db.R — confirms ~/_big/calcofi.org/ctd-viz/ctd-viz.duckdb exists; expected size ~2–4 GB
 (ctd_measurement is the bulk).
 3. R -e 'shiny::runApp("apps/ctd-viz")':
   - Cruise dropdown populates; pick a recent cruise; Load Cruise.
   - Click two stations on the map; Plot Transect.
   - Verify heatmap renders, black sampling dots appear, bathymetry polygon draws from local GEBCO (no network call), and
 marginal histograms show sample-density along both axes.
 4. git status — confirm the five marmap_coord_*.csv files are gone.

 Critical files

 - apps/ctd-viz/prep_db.R (new)
 - apps/ctd-viz/prep_bathy.R (new)
 - apps/ctd-viz/global.R (modify — swap DB source, replace bathy helper, rework plot builder)
 - apps/ctd-viz/server.R (modify — renderPlotly → renderPlot)
 - apps/ctd-viz/ui.R (modify — plotlyOutput → plotOutput)
 - Reference: int-app/prep_db.R, calcofi4r/R/database.R:55-61, workflows/ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd

※ recap: Getting ctd-viz working on a local DuckDB with sampling dots, marginal
   histograms, and GEBCO bathymetry. prep_db.R is materializing ctd_measurement
   (4.4 GB of ~17 GB expected). Next: wait, then launch the Shiny app to       
  verify.

⏺ Plot now reads cleanly — temperature section with contours, semi-transparent
  black sampling dots as thin vertical streaks (one per cast), top histogram
  showing cast distribution along the transect, right histogram showing depth
  distribution of measurements. Bathymetry polygon isn't visible for this
  cruise because all stations are offshore (seafloor >500 m, below the plot's
  max_depth).                                 
                                                  
  Listed 1 directory (ctrl+o to expand)                      
                                                                   
⏺ Done. Summary:                                                               
                                                           
  Artefacts:                                                                   
  - ~/_big/calcofi.org/ctd-viz/`ctd-viz.duckdb` (19.8 GB) — local CTD database 
  - apps/ctd-viz/data/`gebco_calcofi.tif` (1.6 MB) — cropped bathymetry          
  - apps/ctd-viz/`prep_db.R` — rebuilds the DB; materializes partitioned
  ctd_measurement (236M rows) + ctd_summary (105M rows) into native tables;    
  realigns ctd_cast.cruise_key to YYYY-MM-{ship_nodc} format and regenerates   
  ctd_cast_uuid to match ctd_measurement                                       
  - apps/ctd-viz/`prep_bathy.R` — crops GEBCO to the CalCOFI AOI                 
                                                                               
  App changes (global.R, server.R, ui.R):                                      
  - Direct DuckDB read-only connection to the local DB (no more cc_get_db())   
  - get_transect_bathy() uses terra::extract() on the local GEBCO raster       
  (marmap removed)                                                             
  - build_transect_plot() adds small semi-transparent black sampling dots and  
  marginal histograms (sample-density along dist_km + depth_m) via patchwork 
  - Static renderPlot/plotOutput instead of plotly (patchwork composition      
  doesn't survive ggplotly)                                              
  - 5 stale marmap_coord_*.csv files deleted; already ignored by               
  apps/.gitignore                                                 
                                                                               
  Upstream bug found & worked around: the released `ctd_cast.ctd_cast_uuid` was
  MD5'd against old-format `cruise_key` (9802JD) while                           
  `ctd_measurement.ctd_cast_uuid` used new format (1998-02-31JD), so joins
  returned 0 rows. `prep_db.R` now derives the new `cruise_key` from old via       
  `ship.ship_nodc` and regenerates the UUID, producing 5.36M overlapping UUIDs.

  Verification: Shiny app serves HTTP 200; rendered a 10-cast transect for     
  cruise 2026-01-33UD (96,597 measurement rows) to /tmp/ctdviz_transect2.png
  showing the ODV-style section with sampling dots and both marginal           
  histograms. Interactive map + click-to-select still needs a human browser
  pass.

✻ Churned for 16m 4s                   
                                                                    
※ recap: Goal: get the ctd-viz Shiny app running on a local 19.8 GB DuckDB with
   GEBCO bathymetry, sampling dots, and marginal histograms — all tasks are    
  complete and a headless transect render verified. Next: browse               
  http://127.0.0.1:8767 to confirm interactive map click-to-select still works.

The density histograms should be a tiny fraction (like 5%) of overall plot,  
  not 50% on right and 15% top. [Image #1] I should also be able to see Depth  
  (m) labels on y-axis and Distance (km) on x-axis. Adding a few Date labels   
  along x-axis would also be informative as to orient the transect spatially.  
  The land is also impossible and very messed up since there's no way the      
  zig-zag of the survey could produce a linear output. [Image #2] 

⏺ All four fixes in place:                                                     
                                                                               
  1. **Marginals at ~5%** — switched from patchwork (which wouldn't honor width    
  ratios because of axis grobs and legend claims) to ggExtra::ggMarginal(size =
   20) which explicitly sizes the histograms as 1/20th of the main panel.      
   
  2. **Axis labels visible** — "Depth (m)" on left, "Distance (km)" on bottom,     
  scale_y_reverse + scale_x_continuous with proper labs().                     

  3. **Date labels on top** — secondary x-axis via ggplot2::dup_axis() with ~6     
  evenly-spaced breaks mapped to cast datetime_utc (e.g. "07-18 ... 07-29").   

  4. **Bathymetry uses actual cast locations** — get_transect_bathy() now samples  
  GEBCO at each cast lon/lat directly; no densified interpolation that crosses 
  land. Also fixed the sign: this GEBCO tif is positive-down (300m = 300m
  deep), so pmax(depths, 0) not pmax(-depths, 0).                              
                                                                  
  The seafloor polygon will still look jagged when you plot all 49 casts of a  
  zigzag cruise as one "transect" — because consecutive casts really do
  alternate between nearshore (shallow) and offshore (deep) positions.         
  Selecting a smaller, more linear subset (single offshore/onshore arm) will
  give a cleaner section. Rendered /tmp/ctdviz_transect12.png confirms the
  cruise 2025-07-33P4 / all-49-casts / potential_temperature_1 scenario you
  screenshot.

✻ Churned for 11m 14s                   
                                                                    
※ recap: Getting the ctd-viz app working with a local DuckDB, GEBCO bathymetry,
   sampling dots, and marginal histograms. The plot builder now matches all    
  your feedback; next is a live browser pass at http://127.0.0.1:8767 to       
  confirm click-to-transect works end-to-end.

  cruise is selected, drop the "Reset Selection"

  2. Updating the existing dataset to include the invertebrates, and rename it to "CalCOFI Ichthyoplankton Tows"

## 2026-04-08 int-app with inverts

Let's make a branch of the `@../int-app/` repo `db_spatial_invert` for the purposes of wiring in the latest integrated database using a localized copy of the database that should be retrievable with the `@../calcofi4r/` R package from a subset of parquet files that does not include those generated by `@ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast.qmd` (not yet used and unnecessarily big), but does include the newly added parquet tables from `ingest_swfsc_invert.qmd` and `ingest_spatial.qmd` (along with existing `ingest_swfsc_ichthyo.qmd` and `ingest_calcofi_bottle.qmd`). The int-app will need a fair bit of reconfiguration to take advantage of these data tables, which should streamline the process and speed up the queries. The invert data should be unioned with the ichthyo data, perhaps into a materialized view for faster querying so that species there can be queried from the same filter dialog in the app.

←  ☐ Std tally  ☐ Env vars  ☐ Hex GeoJSON  ✔ Submit  →

**Std tally** For the ichthyo+invert union, invert data has `count_per_10m2` (already
standardized) and `count` (raw). Should we use `count_per_10m2` as the
`std_tally` equivalent, paralleling ichthyo's `std_haul_factor * tally /
prop_sorted`?

❯ 1. Use count_per_10m2 (Recommended)
     Map invert count_per_10m2 → std_tally and count → tally. Both represent
  2. Recompute from raw counter area.
     Use invert_net.standard_haul_factor and proportion_sorted to compute

**Env vars** The new bottle_measurement EAV table has 104 measurement types. Should the env 
variable dropdown keep the same 7 variables, or expand to include more?
                                                                         
  1. Keep same 7 for now (Recommended)
     Temperature, salinity, oxygen_umol_kg, phosphate, silicate, nitrite, 
     nitrate — same as current app, just renamed to new measurement_type names.
❯ 2. Expand to ~15 commonly used                                             
     Add chlorophyll_a, phaeopigment, dynamic_height, sigma_theta, pressure, 
     par, pH, ammonia to the dropdown.

**Hex GeoJSON** The new site table lacks hex_h3res columns (they were pre-computed in the old  
DB). We need to compute them at app init. Should we also regenerate hex.geojson
 (44MB) on first run, or keep the existing file?                         
                                      
  1. Regenerate if missing/stale (Recommended)                            
     Check hex.geojson freshness against db_version; regenerate on mismatch.   
     Adds ~10s to first cold start but ensures consistency.                  
  2. Always regenerate at init                                               
     Delete and rebuild hex.geojson every app restart. Slower startup but 
     guarantees correctness.                                         
  3. Keep existing hex.geojson                                                 
     Assume the current file is compatible. Fastest startup but may have stale 
     hexagons if site data changes.                                            
❯ 4. Let's regenerate the `hex.geojson` for completeness, but do this beforehand as a preparatory step. Also download all the parquet files locally (to be run on server), ideally by expanding the function `cc_get_db()` if necessary to have it handle the parquet file downloads if locally missing or outdated compared to remote GCS. Also pre-generate the h3 indexes and anything else that would could dramatically speed up the launch and querying within the app. Keep the augmentation of the database such that we can easily update it with new parquet files and then run a single function to get it ready for the app.

 Here is Claude's plan:                                                        

 **Plan: Wire int-app to calcofi4r parquet DB with invert + spatial data**
                                                        
 Context         

 The int-app Shiny application currently downloads a monolithic `calcofi.duckdb`
  from `file.calcofi.io` containing an OLD schema (tables: species, larva, net,
 tow, site, cast, bottle, taxonomy). The CalCOFI pipeline has migrated to a
 parquet-based release system served via `calcofi4r::cc_get_db()`, with a new
 normalized schema. The latest release (`v2026.04.06`) includes 29 tables
 including newly ingested invertebrate and spatial data.

 Key discovery: 54 of 60 invert species match the species table by
 `scientific_name`, and 58 of 60 match by `itis_tsn` = `itis_id`. All matched
 species have `worms_id` and `species_id`, so taxonomy children lookup works for
 inverts too.

 Goals:
 1. Switch int-app to a locally materialized DuckDB built from parquets via
 `calcofi4r`
 2. Pre-join and pre-sort materialized tables (`bio_obs`, `env_obs`) to eliminate
 runtime joins
 3. Pre-compute H3 hex indices, `quarter`, `std_tally`, and `hex.geojson` in prep
 step
 4. Union ichthyo + invert data with full species/taxonomy linkage
 5. Expand env variables to ~15 commonly used measurement types
 6. Single `prep_db.R` script to rebuild everything when data updates
 ---
 **Step 1: Create branch in int-app**

 ```bash
 cd /Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/int-app
 git checkout -b db_spatial_invert
 ```

 ---
 **Step 2: Add local_data + tables params to cc_get_db() in calcofi4r**

 File: `/Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/calcofi4r/R/database.R` (lines 43-180)

 New signature:
 ```r
 cc_get_db <- function(
     version     = "latest",
     local_cache = TRUE,
     cache_dir   = NULL,
     refresh     = FALSE,
     local_data  = FALSE,   # NEW: download parquets + create local tables
     tables      = NULL)    # NEW: subset of tables to include (NULL = all)
 ```

 When `local_data = TRUE`:
 - Download each non-partitioned parquet to
 `{cache_dir}/parquet/{version}/{table_name}.parquet` via
 `.cc_download_gcs_file()`
 - Skip download if local file exists and size matches `catalog$tables$rows`
 (unless `refresh = TRUE`)
 - `CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE` from local parquet (not VIEW)
 - When `tables` is non-NULL, only process tables in that vector
 - Partitioned tables: if included, download all partition files via S3
 directory listing

 The `tables` filter applies to both `local_data = TRUE` and `FALSE` paths, so views
  can also be subset.

 ---
 **Step 3: Create `prep_db.R` in int-app**
 New file: `/Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/int-app/prep_db.R`

 Single idempotent script to build the fully optimized local app database.
 Re-run when new release is available.

 **3a. Download parquets + create base tables**

 ```r
 # exclude CTD (too large, not yet used in app)
 exclude_tables <- c("ctd_cast", "ctd_measurement", "ctd_summary", "ctd_wide",
                      "dic_sample", "dic_measurement", "dic_summary",
                      "cruise_summary")
 con <- cc_get_db(
   version    = db_version,
   cache_dir  = db_dir,
   local_data = TRUE,
   tables     = setdiff(all_tables, exclude_tables))
 ```

 **3b. H3 augmented tables (presorted by finest H3)**

 All three tables get H3 columns at resolutions 1-10 and are sorted by
 `hex_h3res10` for optimal `GROUP BY` performance on the finest resolution
 (coarser resolutions benefit from the hierarchical H3 cell ordering).

 ```sql
 -- site_h3: 61K rows, sorted by hex_h3res10
 CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE site_h3 AS
 SELECT *,
   h3_latlng_to_cell(latitude, longitude, 1)::BIGINT  AS hex_h3res1,
   h3_latlng_to_cell(latitude, longitude, 2)::BIGINT  AS hex_h3res2,
   ...
   h3_latlng_to_cell(latitude, longitude, 10)::BIGINT AS hex_h3res10
 FROM site
 ORDER BY hex_h3res10;

 -- invert_net_h3: 7.5K rows, sorted by hex_h3res10
 CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE invert_net_h3 AS
 SELECT *,
   h3_latlng_to_cell(latitude, longitude, 1)::BIGINT  AS hex_h3res1,
   ...
   h3_latlng_to_cell(latitude, longitude, 10)::BIGINT AS hex_h3res10
 FROM invert_net
 ORDER BY hex_h3res10;

 -- casts_h3: 36K rows, sorted by hex_h3res10
 CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE casts_h3 AS
 SELECT *,
   h3_latlng_to_cell(lat_dec, lon_dec, 1)::BIGINT  AS hex_h3res1,
   ...
   h3_latlng_to_cell(lat_dec, lon_dec, 10)::BIGINT AS hex_h3res10
 FROM casts
 ORDER BY hex_h3res10;
 ```

 **3c. bio_obs materialized table**

 Pre-joins ichthyo (5-table chain: ichthyo -> species -> net -> tow ->
 site_h3) and invert (3-table chain: invert -> species -> invert_net_h3).
 Pre-computes `std_tally`, `quarter`, `name`. Sorted by `scientific_name`, `time_start`
 for species filtering + temporal queries.

 Invert species get `species_id` and `worms_id` via LEFT JOIN to species on
 `itis_tsn = itis_id` (58/60 match). The 2 unmatched species ("Abraliopsis sp
 A", "Onychoteuthis sp B") fall through with NULL `worms_id` but still have
 `scientific_name` for direct filtering.

 ```sql
 CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE bio_obs AS

 -- ICHTHYO: ~400K rows (total count rows only, excludes stage/size detail)
 SELECT
   'ichthyo'           AS source,
   sp.scientific_name,
   sp.common_name,
   sp.species_id,
   sp.worms_id,
   i.tally,
   n.std_haul_factor * i.tally / NULLIF(n.prop_sorted, 0) AS std_tally,
   t.time_start,
   sh.longitude,
   sh.latitude,
   EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM t.time_start)::INTEGER AS quarter,
   sh.hex_h3res1, sh.hex_h3res2, sh.hex_h3res3, sh.hex_h3res4, sh.hex_h3res5,
   sh.hex_h3res6, sh.hex_h3res7, sh.hex_h3res8, sh.hex_h3res9, sh.hex_h3res10
 FROM ichthyo i
 JOIN species sp ON i.species_id = sp.species_id
 JOIN net n      ON i.net_uuid   = n.net_uuid
 JOIN tow t      ON n.tow_uuid   = t.tow_uuid
 JOIN site_h3 sh ON t.site_uuid  = sh.site_uuid
 WHERE i.tally IS NOT NULL
   AND i.measurement_type IS NULL

 UNION ALL

 -- INVERT: ~8K rows (count_per_10m2 as std_tally)
 SELECT
   'invert'            AS source,
   inv.scientific_name,
   COALESCE(sp.common_name, inv.common_name) AS common_name,
   sp.species_id,                           -- from species table (58/60
 match)
   sp.worms_id,                             -- enables taxonomy children for
 inverts
   inv_c.measurement_value::INTEGER AS tally,
   inv.measurement_value AS std_tally,      -- count_per_10m2 already
 standardized
   inh.time_start,
   inh.longitude,
   inh.latitude,
   EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM inh.time_start)::INTEGER AS quarter,
   inh.hex_h3res1, inh.hex_h3res2, inh.hex_h3res3, inh.hex_h3res4,
 inh.hex_h3res5,
   inh.hex_h3res6, inh.hex_h3res7, inh.hex_h3res8, inh.hex_h3res9,
 inh.hex_h3res10
 FROM invert inv
 JOIN invert_net_h3 inh ON inv.invert_net_id = inh.invert_net_id
 LEFT JOIN species sp   ON inv.itis_tsn::INTEGER = sp.itis_id
 LEFT JOIN invert inv_c ON inv.invert_net_id = inv_c.invert_net_id
   AND inv.scientific_name = inv_c.scientific_name
   AND inv_c.measurement_type = 'count'
 WHERE inv.measurement_type = 'count_per_10m2'
   AND inv.measurement_value IS NOT NULL

 ORDER BY scientific_name, time_start;
 ```

 **3d. env_obs materialized table**

 Pre-joins `casts_h3 -> bottle -> bottle_measurement` for the 15 common
 measurement types. Pre-computes quarter. Sorted by `measurement_type`,
 `datetime_utc` for fast filtered scans.

 This eliminates the 3-table join in `get_env()` — runtime query is just a
 filtered scan on one table.

 ```sql
 CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE env_obs AS
 SELECT
   c.cast_id,
   c.datetime_utc,
   EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM c.datetime_utc)::INTEGER AS quarter,
   c.lat_dec,
   c.lon_dec,
   b.bottle_id,
   b.depth_m,
   bm.measurement_type,
   bm.measurement_value AS qty,
   c.hex_h3res1, c.hex_h3res2, c.hex_h3res3, c.hex_h3res4, c.hex_h3res5,
   c.hex_h3res6, c.hex_h3res7, c.hex_h3res8, c.hex_h3res9, c.hex_h3res10
 FROM casts_h3 c
 JOIN bottle b            ON c.cast_id = b.cast_id
 JOIN bottle_measurement bm ON b.bottle_id = bm.bottle_id
 WHERE bm.measurement_type IN (
   'temperature', 'salinity', 'oxygen_umol_kg', 'phosphate', 'silicate',
   'nitrite', 'nitrate', 'chlorophyll_a', 'phaeopigment', 'dynamic_height',
   'sigma_theta', 'pressure', 'par', 'ph', 'ammonia')
   AND bm.measurement_value IS NOT NULL
 ORDER BY bm.measurement_type, c.datetime_utc;
 ```

 **3e. Generate hex.geojson**

 Union distinct H3 cells from `site_h3` and `invert_net_h3`, convert to boundary
 WKT, write as GeoJSON. Includes cells from both ichthyo and invert sampling
 locations.

 **3f. Drop intermediate source tables (saves disk)**

 After building materialized views, drop raw tables no longer needed by the
 app:

 ```sql
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS ichthyo;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS net;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS tow;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS site;        -- replaced by site_h3
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS invert;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS invert_net;  -- replaced by invert_net_h3
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS casts;       -- replaced by casts_h3
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS bottle;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS bottle_measurement;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS cast_condition;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS segment;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS grid;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS cruise;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS ship;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS lookup;
 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS dataset;
 ```

 Keep these tables (needed at runtime):
 - `species` — species name + worms_id lookup for get_sp() and species dropdown
 - `taxon` — taxonomy tree for get_taxon_children() / get_taxon_parentage()
 - `taxa_rank` — rank ordering for tree display
 - `measurement_type` — measurement descriptions (optional, for tooltips)
 - `bio_obs` — materialized species/invert data
 - `env_obs` — materialized environmental data
 - `site_h3` — H3-augmented sites (used for hex.geojson generation)
 - `invert_net_h3` — H3-augmented invert nets (used for hex.geojson generation)
 - `casts_h3` — H3-augmented casts (spatial filter reference)

 **Step 4: Rewrite global.R**

 **4a. DB connection** (replace lines 19-63)

 Remove: `calcofi_db`, `local_db`, `local_db_srv`, `tmp_db`, `is_remote_newer()`, all
 `use_local_db` logic.

 ```r
 db_path <- here("data/calcofi_app.duckdb")
 stopifnot("Run prep_db.R first" = file.exists(db_path))
 drv <- duckdb(dbdir = db_path, read_only = TRUE)
 con <- dbConnect(drv)
 dbExecute(con, "LOAD h3;")
 dbExecute(con, "LOAD spatial;")
 ```

 **4b. Species names** (replace lines 119-126)

 Build combined list from ichthyo species (with taxonomy rank) + invert
 species (with "invert:" tag). Since 58/60 invert species are in `species`, most
  will appear in both lists — `unique()` deduplicates. Show invert-specific
 label only for species not already in ichthyo list.

 ```r
 ichthyo_names <- tbl(con, "species") |>
   left_join(
     tbl(con, "taxon") |> filter(authority == "worms"),
     by = join_by(worms_id == taxonID)) |>
   mutate(
     name = paste0(common_name, " (", tolower(taxonRank), ": ",
 scientific_name, ")")) |>
   pull(name)

 # invert species not already in ichthyo list
 invert_only <- tbl(con, "bio_obs") |>
   filter(source == "invert") |>
   distinct(scientific_name, common_name) |>
   collect() |>
   anti_join(
     tbl(con, "species") |> collect(),
     by = "scientific_name") |>
   mutate(
     name = paste0(common_name, " (invert: ", scientific_name, ")")) |>
   pull(name)

 sp_names <- c(ichthyo_names, invert_only) |> sort()
 ```

 ```r
 bio_date_rng <- tbl(con, "bio_obs") |>
   summarize(
     date_min = min(time_start, na.rm = TRUE) |> as.Date(),
     date_max = max(time_start, na.rm = TRUE) |> as.Date()) |>
   collect() |> as.vector()

 env_date_rng <- tbl(con, "env_obs") |>
   summarize(
     date_min = min(datetime_utc, na.rm = TRUE) |> as.Date(),
     date_max = max(datetime_utc, na.rm = TRUE) |> as.Date()) |>
   collect() |> as.vector()

 min_max_date <- c(
   min(bio_date_rng[[1]], env_date_rng[[1]]),
   max(bio_date_rng[[2]], env_date_rng[[2]]))
 ```

 ---
 **Step 5: Rewrite `get_sp()` in `functions.R`**

 File: `/Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/int-app/app/functions.R` (lines 147-241)

 Queries `bio_obs` directly — no joins needed at runtime. Since invert species
 have `worms_id` in `bio_obs` (via `species` table linkage in Step 3c), taxonomy
 children expansion works for both sources.

 ```r
 get_sp <- function(sp_name, qtr, date_range, ck_children = TRUE) {

   # resolve selected names to worms_ids via species + taxon tables
   if (ck_children) {
     df_sel <- tbl(con, "species") |>
       left_join(
         tbl(con, "taxon") |> filter(authority == "worms"),
         by = join_by(worms_id == taxonID)) |>
       mutate(
         name = paste0(common_name, " (", tolower(taxonRank), ": ",
 scientific_name, ")")) |>
       filter(name %in% sp_name) |>
       select(name, worms_id) |>
       collect() |>
       mutate(children = map(worms_id, get_taxon_children, con = con)) |>
       unnest(children)
     worms_ids <- unique(df_sel$acceptedNameUsageID)
   } else {
     worms_ids <- tbl(con, "species") |>
       left_join(
         tbl(con, "taxon") |> filter(authority == "worms"),
         by = join_by(worms_id == taxonID)) |>
       mutate(
         name = paste0(common_name, " (", tolower(taxonRank), ": ",
 scientific_name, ")")) |>
       filter(name %in% sp_name) |>
       pull(worms_id)
   }

   # query bio_obs (both ichthyo + invert, pre-joined + pre-sorted)
   tbl(con, "bio_obs") |>
     filter(
       worms_id %in% worms_ids,
       between(time_start, !!date_range[1], !!date_range[2]),
       quarter %in% qtr) |>
     mutate(
       name = paste0(common_name, " (", scientific_name, ")"))
 }
 ```

 Key simplifications:
 - Works identically for ichthyo and invert (both have worms_id in bio_obs)
 - No separate invert name parsing needed (58/60 match species table)
 - std_tally, quarter, H3 columns all pre-computed
 - For the 2 unmatched invert species: they'll have NULL worms_id in bio_obs
 and won't appear in species dropdown (acceptable — they're rare variants like
  "Abraliopsis sp A")

 ---
 **Step 6: Rewrite `get_env()` in `functions.R`**

 File: `/Users/bbest/Github/CalCOFI/int-app/app/functions.R` (lines 293-335)

 Queries `env_obs` directly — no joins needed at runtime.

 ```r
 get_env <- function(env_var, qtr, date_range, min_depth, max_depth) {
   tbl(con, "env_obs") |>
     filter(
       measurement_type == env_var,
       !is.na(qty),
       between(depth_m, min_depth, max_depth),
       between(datetime_utc, !!date_range[1], !!date_range[2]),
       quarter %in% qtr) |>
     rename(dtime = datetime_utc) |>
     select(dtime, cast_id, depth_m, lat_dec, lon_dec, qty,
            starts_with("hex_h3res"))
 }
 ```

 Key simplifications:
 - Single-table filtered scan (pre-sorted by `measurement_type`, `datetime_utc`)
 - quarter pre-computed, no `sql()` call needed
 - dtime is just a rename of `datetime_utc`
 - No site join needed (H3 columns on `env_obs` directly)

⏺ Here's a summary of all changes made:                                        
                                                                             
  **Changes complete across 2 repos**
                                                                               
  **calcofi4r** (`R/database.R`)
                                                                               
  - Added local_data and tables parameters to `cc_get_db()`                      
  - When `local_data = TRUE`: downloads parquet files locally, creates TABLE
  instead of VIEW                                                              
  - When `tables` is non-NULL: filters catalog to only include specified tables
  - Updated roxygen docs with new params and examples                          
                                                                  
  **int-app** (new branch `db_spatial_invert`)                                       
                                                                  
  **prep_db.R** (new) — single script to build the optimized local database:       
  - Downloads parquets via `cc_get_db(local_data = TRUE)`, excluding CTD/DIC
  - Builds H3-augmented tables (`site_h3, invert_net_h3, casts_h3`) sorted by    
  `hex_h3res10`                                                              
  - Creates `bio_obs` — pre-joined ichthyo + invert union with species linkage   
  (58/60 match via `itis_tsn`), pre-computed `std_tally`, `quarter`, `H3` columns   
  - Creates `env_obs` — pre-joined casts + bottle + bottle_measurement for 15 env
   types                                                                       
  - Drops intermediate tables to save disk                                     
  - Generates `hex.geojson` from both ichthyo and invert sampling locations
                                                                               
  **app/global.R** — simplified DB connection:                                     
  - Connects to pre-built local DuckDB (read-only)                             
  - Combined species dropdown (ichthyo + invert from species table)            
  - Date ranges from `bio_obs` and `env_obs`                                       
  - Expanded to 15 environmental variable choices                              
                                                                               
  **app/functions.R** — query simplification:                                      
  - `get_sp()` → queries `bio_obs` directly (no 5-table join chain)                
  - `get_env()` → queries `env_obs` directly (no 3-table join chain)               
  - `taxonomy` → `taxon` everywhere                                   
  - `prep_splot()` column renames (`cast_id`, `depth_m`)                             
                                                                                
  **app/server.R** — column/default updates:                                       
  - Default env var "temperature" (was "t_deg_c")                              
  - `depthm` → `depth_m`, `cst_cnt` → `cast_id`                                        
  - Removed `is_server`/`local_db` references                         
                                                                               
  Next steps                                                                   
                                                                               
  1. Run `Rscript prep_db.R` in `int-app/` to build the local database             
  2. Launch app with `shiny::runApp("app")` to test

Let's fix the root problem with failure of GCS upload. Ah, there is no `ingest/_spatial` folder in GCS bucket so think problem is that `@../workflows/ingest_spatial.qmd` failed to upload to GCS and should've done so, but `@../workflows/release_database.qmd` should've also failed if expected table not present for copying. Let's similarly keep `prep_db.R` as is -- I want it too to fail if not finding expected parquet files.

Maybe `@../workflows/ingest_spatial.qmd` GCS upload code now present, but not properly invalidated for `tar_make()` after edit and simply needs to be re-run.

⏺ Here's the summary:              
                                                                               
  Root cause: `ingest_spatial.qmd` uploads PMTiles to                            
  `gs://calcofi-files-public/_spatial/` but was missing the parquet upload to
  `gs://calcofi-db/ingest/spatial/`. When `release_database.qmd` tried to          
  server-side copy from `gs://calcofi-db/ingest/spatial/_spatial.parquet`, the
  `system2()` call failed silently (exit code wasn't checked).

  Two fixes:                                                                   
  
  1. `ingest_spatial.qmd` (line 536): Added `sync_to_gcs()` call to upload parquets
   to `gs://calcofi-db/ingest/spatial/`, matching the pattern used by all other
  ingest notebooks.                                                            
  2. `release_database.qmd` (line 652): Added exit code checking on `system2()` GCS
   copy — now `stop()`s with a clear error message if any copy fails, instead of 
  silently continuing.
                                                                               
  To get the spatial parquets into GCS and fix the current release, you'll need
   to re-run the upload-gcs chunk in `ingest_spatial.qmd` (or the full
  `tar_make()`), then re-run the release upload for `_spatial` and `_spatial_attr`.

Actually I don't think that path is right for the GCS destination.
Shouldn't there be a datetimestamp in the path? 


⏺ The sync_to_gcs() in mirror mode (non-archive) syncs flat — it just mirrors  
  local_dir to gs://{bucket}/{gcs_prefix}/. No timestamps. So the upload code I
   added is correct — sync_to_gcs(local_dir = dir_parquet, gcs_prefix =        
  "ingest/spatial", bucket = "calcofi-db") will produce                        
  gs://calcofi-db/ingest/spatial/_spatial.parquet etc., matching the flat      
  pattern used by all other ingests.                                           
                                                                  
  The timestamped directories you see are in                                   
  calcofi-files-public/archive/_spatial/ — that's the archive call earlier in
  the chunk (line 88-94), which uses archive = FALSE but the path has          
  historical snapshots from previous manual runs. The archive paths are a
  separate concern from the ingest upload.

  The fix is correct as-is. You can re-run the upload-gcs chunk now (with      
  bebest@ucsd.edu active) and it should upload _spatial.parquet,
  _spatial_attr.parquet, manifest.json, and metadata.json to                   
  gs://calcofi-db/ingest/spatial/.

  seeing the release for today nor the updated parquet files in the `ingest/`

  `calcofi4db::build_targets_list()`?

⏺ Now it works — ingest_calcofi_ctd_cast is excluded (8 targets instead of 9),
  and it's also removed from the release_database auto-dependencies. You can   
  now run:                                                                     
        
  targets::tar_invalidate(everything())                                        
  targets::tar_make()                                             
                                                                               
  This will re-run all ingests (ichthyo, bottle, invert, spatial, dic) and the 
  release, skipping CTD.

⏺ Updated plan                                                             
  ⎿  /plan to preview                                                          

 Ready to code?                                                                
                                                                               
 Here is Claude's plan:                                                        

 **Plan: Fold Ed Weber's inverts into ichthyo pipeline + simplify int-app**

 Context

 Ed Weber added `inverts.csv` (11,815 rows: `netid`, `sppcode`, `tally`) to the SWFSC
 ichthyo database. This replaces the ERDDAP-based `ingest_swfsc_invert` workflow
  entirely. The inverts share the same net/tow/site structure as ichthyo and
 use species codes already in the species table.

 This simplifies the int-app: no more separate invert tables, UNION queries,
 or `invert_net_h3`. The `bio_obs` materialized table becomes a single query on
 the `ichthyo` table (which now includes egg, larva, AND invert rows).

 ---
 **Step 1: Add inverts to redefinition metadata**

 `workflows/metadata/swfsc/ichthyo/tbls_redefine.csv` — add:
 ```
 inverts,invert,number of invertebrates from net tow by species
 ```

 `workflows/metadata/swfsc/ichthyo/flds_redefine.csv` — add:
 ```
 inverts,invert,netid,net_uuid,character,uuid,1,1,,,,
 inverts,invert,sppcode,species_id,numeric,smallint,2,2,"CalCOFI species
 identifier, primary key in species table",,,
 inverts,invert,tally,tally,numeric,integer,3,3,Raw count of
 invertebrates,count,,
 ```

 Step 2: Add invert to consolidate_ichthyo_tables() in calcofi4db

 File: calcofi4db/R/wrangle.R (line 778)

 - Add invert_tbl = "invert" parameter
 - Add section 6 after larva_size block (line 895):
 ```r
   # 6. invert totals (life_stage = 'invert', measurement_type = NULL)
   if (table_exists(invert_tbl)) {
     invert_data <- dplyr::tbl(con, invert_tbl) |>
       dplyr::collect() |>
       dplyr::mutate(
         net_uuid          = .data[[net_id_col]],
         life_stage        = "invert",
         measurement_type  = NA_character_,
         measurement_value = NA_real_) |>
       select_with_prov(
         "net_uuid", "species_id", "life_stage",
         "measurement_type", "measurement_value", "tally")
     ichthyo_rows <- append(ichthyo_rows, list(invert_data))
     message(glue::glue("  Processed {nrow(invert_data)} rows from
 {invert_tbl}"))
   }
 ```
  
 **Step 3: Simplify int-app `prep_db.R`**

 Since inverts are now in the `ichthyo` table (via consolidation), the `bio_obs`
 query simplifies to a single SELECT (no UNION, no separate invert tables):

 ```sql
 CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE bio_obs AS
 SELECT
   i.life_stage AS source,       -- 'egg', 'larva', or 'invert'
   sp.scientific_name,
   sp.common_name,
   sp.species_id,
   sp.worms_id,
   i.tally,
   n.std_haul_factor * i.tally / NULLIF(n.prop_sorted, 0) AS std_tally,
   t.time_start,
   sh.longitude,
   sh.latitude,
   EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM t.time_start)::INTEGER AS quarter,
   sh.hex_h3res1, ..., sh.hex_h3res10
 FROM ichthyo i
 JOIN species sp ON i.species_id = sp.species_id
 JOIN net n      ON i.net_uuid   = n.net_uuid
 JOIN tow t      ON n.tow_uuid   = t.tow_uuid
 JOIN site_h3 sh ON t.site_uuid  = sh.site_uuid
 WHERE i.tally IS NOT NULL
   AND i.measurement_type IS NULL
 ORDER BY sp.scientific_name, t.time_start
 ```
 Other prep_db.R changes:
 - Remove invert_net_h3 table creation (no longer needed)
 - Remove invert_net and invert from drop list (they won't exist)
 - Remove invert_net_h3 from hex.geojson UNION (only site_h3)
 - Remove invert_net_h3 from kept-tables list

 **Step 4: Simplify int-app `global.R` species names**

 Since all invert species are in the species table with worms_id, the separate
  invert_only name list is no longer needed:
 ```r
 sp_names <- tbl(con, "species") |>
   left_join(
     tbl(con, "taxon") |> filter(authority == "worms"),
     by = join_by(worms_id == taxonID)) |>
   mutate(
     name = paste0(common_name, " (", tolower(taxonRank), ": ",
 scientific_name, ")")) |>
   pull(name) |>
   sort()
 ```
 No more invert_only / anti_join / "(invert: ...)" display names.

 **Step 5: Delete `ingest_swfsc_invert.qmd`**

 - Delete `workflows/ingest_swfsc_invert.qmd`
 - Delete `_output/ingest_swfsc_invert.html`
 - Remove `data/parquet/swfsc_invert/` directory
 - The `_targets` pipeline will naturally exclude it (no qmd = no target)

 **Step 6: Remove from exclude list in `_targets.R`**

 Remove "ingest_swfsc_invert" from any exclude lists. Only CTD remains
 excluded:
 ```r
 build_targets_list(exclude = "ingest_calcofi_ctd-cast")
 ```

 ---
 Files to modify

⏺ App launches and serves HTTP 200. Summary of fixes:                          
                                                                               
  Fixed: authority == "worms" → "WoRMS" (case mismatch) in global.R,           
  functions.R (both dbplyr filters and SQL function defaults). This was causing
   all taxonRank to be NULL → the (: sad face pattern and the sardine default  
  not matching.                                                   

  Not a code bug — data gap: Market squid (Doryteuthis opalescens, species_id  
  9542) is not in Ed Weber's species table. The inverts.csv has 8,132
  observations across ~60 species but Doryteuthis isn't one of them. This needs
   to be raised with Ed.                                          

  Pre-existing: The Shiny client errors about "map output recalculating/idle"  
  are a known mapgl timing issue, not introduced by our changes.

The majority of the inverts data should be market squid. When looking at the ERDDAP dataset online, the `calcofi_species_code` is `9542` for "Market squid" (_Doryteuthis opalescens_) -- does that really not exist in Ed's `invert.csv` or is it just not in the `species` table?

https://oceanview.pfeg.noaa.gov/erddap/tabledap/erdCalCOFIinvcnt.htmlTable?cruise%2Cship%2Cship_code%2Corder_occupied%2Ctow_type%2Ctow_number%2Cnet_location%2Ctime%2Clatitude%2Clongitude%2Cline%2Cstation%2Cstandard_haul_factor%2Cvolume_sampled%2Cproportion_sorted%2Cscientific_name%2Ccommon_name%2Citis_tsn%2Ccalcofi_species_code%2Cinvert_count%2Cinverts_10m2%2Cinverts_100m3&time%3E=2015-07-01&time%3C=2023-01-25T09%3A53%3A00Z&common_name=%22Market%20squid%22

⏺ Sppcode 9542 is in Ed's inverts.csv (1,080 rows — 3rd most common). The
  problem is that species_id = 9542 doesn't exist in the species table. The
  species table has 9540 (Teuthida, the parent order) but not 9542 (Doryteuthis
   opalescens specifically).

  The consolidate_ichthyo_tables() → bio_obs uses JOIN species sp ON
  i.species_id = sp.species_id — an INNER JOIN. So inverts with sppcode 9542
  are silently dropped because there's no matching species row.

   and before bio_obs is built. The 6 known species (including Market squid) will be INSERTed into the species
  table if not already present, so the INNER JOIN in bio_obs will pick them up.

  The 4 unknown codes (9563, 9598, 9785, 9791 — 650 rows total) will still be dropped until Ed confirms what they
   are.

  You can now re-run tar_make() to rebuild the pipeline with Market squid included.

⏺ The tour is showing because is_tour_on is checked in server.R, not in global.R where it's defined. Let me check:

   site point with ST_Point(lon,lat) doesn't help because the grid.geom it intersects against is still broken.

  The grid is loaded from hex WKB via ST_GeomFromHEXWKB(). The bug is specifically in how DuckDB v1.5.1 spatial
  stores polygon WKB after deserialization. Let me fix the assign_grid_key function to re-parse the grid geometry
  on-the-fly:

⏺ The grid geometry works fine in a fresh DB in the same session. The corruption only happens in the ichthyo

   heavy processing. Let me check: if we reload the grid fresh on the existing corrupt DB, does it fix?

  grid creation and the spatial join. The fix: add a CHECKPOINT + reload of the grid geometry right before
  assign_grid_key.

  cli/rlang intercepting {grid_table} in a glue string. The issue is that when glue::glue() is used inside a

   dbListFields() + paste0() to avoid glue interpolation entirely.

This "grid geometry refresh workaround" in calcofi4db v2.5.6 feels a bit hacky. I think there may be issues with DuckDB writing parquet files with compatibility at version 1.0 but to get the new fully native GEOMETRY data type, we need to impose DuckDB version 1.5.1 (latest) or higher. Then we back out this extraneous geom_wkb and use the native storage and spatial capabilities. See documentation at https://duckdb.org/docs/current/internals/storage

⏺ There it is. DuckDB v1.5.1 is running but the storage compatibility version is v0.10.2 — that's the default for backward compat. The native GEOMETRY type was
  introduced in v1.5, but the storage format is writing at v0.10.2 level where GEOMETRY was an extension type with different serialization.                     


⏺ Updated plan
  ⎿  /plan to preview

 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:

 **Fix DuckDB Spatial: Use Native `GEOMETRY` via `storage_compatibility_version`**

 Context

 The "grid geometry refresh from geom_wkb" workaround in calcofi4db v2.5.6
 is hacky. The real root cause: DuckDB v1.5.1 defaults to
 `storage_compatibility_version = 'v0.10.2'` for backward compat. At that
 storage version, GEOMETRY is serialized using the old spatial extension
 format, which has a deserialization bug (_Buffer overflow / Skipping beyond end of binary data_).

 **DuckDB `v1.5` made `GEOMETRY` a built-in native type**. Setting
 `storage_compatibility_version = 'latest'` (storage version 68) uses the new
 native serialization, which works correctly — confirmed with 61K points +
 218 grid polygons + heavy workload, no corruption.

 Changes

 1. Set `storage_compatibility_version = 'latest'` in `get_duckdb_con()`

 File: calcofi4db/R/duckdb.R

 The existing code already sets `autoload_known_extensions = "true"` in the
 default config. Add `storage_compatibility_version = "latest"`:

 ```r
 default_config <- list(
   autoload_known_extensions    = "true",
   storage_compatibility_version = "latest"
 )
 ```

 This applies to ALL wrangling DBs created by `get_duckdb_con()`.

 2. Remove the `geom_wkb` workaround from `assign_grid_key()`

 File: calcofi4db/R/spatial.R

 Remove the grid geometry refresh block (the `geom_wkb` check and
 `ST_GeomFromHEXWKB` re-parse). With native `GEOMETRY` storage, this is
 unnecessary.

 3. Drop `geom_wkb` / `geom_ctr_wkb` columns in ichthyo QMD

 File: workflows/ingest_swfsc_ichthyo.qmd

 Restore the DROP COLUMN `geom_wkb` / DROP COLUMN `geom_ctr_wkb` lines that were
  removed in the workaround.

 4. Delete stale wrangling DBs

 All existing wrangling DBs were created with v0.10.2 storage. They need to
 be rebuilt with the new storage version:

 ```bash
 rm -f data/wrangling/*.duckdb data/wrangling/*.wal
 ```

 Files to modify

 ┌────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┐
 │                File                │              Change              │
 ├────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
 │                                    │ Add                              │
 │ calcofi4db/R/duckdb.R              │ storage_compatibility_version =  │
 │                                    │ "latest" to default config       │
 ├────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
 │                                    │ Remove geom_wkb refresh          │
 │ calcofi4db/R/spatial.R             │ workaround from                  │
 │                                    │ assign_grid_key()                │
 ├────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
 │ workflows/ingest_swfsc_ichthyo.qmd │ Restore DROP COLUMN geom_wkb /   │
 │                                    │ geom_ctr_wkb                     │
 └────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┘

 Verification

 1. devtools::document() + devtools::install() calcofi4db
 2. Delete all wrangling DBs
 3. targets::tar_invalidate(everything()) +
 targets::tar_make("ingest_calcofi_dic")
 4. Confirm ichthyo passes chunk 56 (update_site_from_grid) without the
 refresh workaround
 5. Verify: SELECT current_setting('storage_compatibility_version') returns
 'latest' on any wrangling DB

`redo_if_exists <- TRUE` (`@ingest_spatial.qmd`) or `overwrite <- TRUE` (`@ingest_swfsc_ichthyo.qmd`) equivalent for all the calcofi QMDs --         
actually we should standardize on this so same flag with all QMDs, or perhaps a source a new file libs/ingest.R or similar to set flags and paths    
common to ingest scripts?
